notes: Two fixes following the one-time-via-Journal build. ISSUE 1 — Budget page now has a dedicated 'One-time expenses' tube. For the selected month it sums all journal one-time expenses (category 'oneTime') and renders a row with an intentional 0 budget, so any spend reads as fully 'over' (full red tube, shows 'actual / ₹0 · over budget'). The row only appears in months that have a one-time spend, is click-to-expand (new 'onetime' details filter lists the underlying transactions), and one-time transactions are explicitly excluded from the 'All discretionary' drilldown so they never leak into that total. renderBudgetRow gained a zeroIsOver flag driving the 0-budget-is-over rendering; all existing rows are unaffected (flag defaults falsy). ISSUE 2 — logging a one-time expense paid FROM savings was wrongly shrinking the 'Planned to Savings' ACTUAL. The actual is computed as net savings inflow and was subtracting expenses paid from savings; so a ₹60k advance transfer became ₹15k after a ₹45k one-time. Fixed: one-time expenses (category 'oneTime') no longer reduce the planned-savings actual. Rationale: a one-time expense is a spending event — it already reduces the TARGET via surplus (₹71.1k → ₹26k, correct) and appears in Total Expenses; it must not also reduce the deliberate transfer the user actually made. The row now reads e.g. '₹60k of ₹26k' — plan reduced by the one-time, transfer shown as-is. Regular expenses paid from savings keep the established net-inflow behavior (only the new one-time category is excluded); redemption/income credits from the v.79 fix are untouched. Both changes are display-only (Budget-page tube + projection-sheet planned-savings actual); engine net-worth, projectSavingsChain, and Total Expenses/target math are unchanged, and the savings balance still reflects a one-time-from-savings debit via the reverse-walk. KNOWN ITEM (still deferred): current-month savings forecast uses planned surplus. Runtime check still open: one-time expense debit correctness for credit-card/custom accounts. SELF_TEST.js and PROPAGATION_TEST_{1,2,3}.js remain valid.
